name: Koloketes (Pumpkin Pies)
recipeInstructions:
-----------------------------------PASTRY-----------------------------------
4 cups Plain flour
1/2 teaspoon Salt
3/4 cup Peanut or corn oil
1/2 cup Cold water
3 tablespoons Lemon juice
Beaten egg and milk for glaze
Makes: 30 Pies Oven temperature: 200°C (400°F) reducing to 180°C (350°F)
Cooking time: 30 minutes
1. Peel the pumpkin, remove seeds, and cut into 5 mm (1/4 inch) dice.
2. Measure and place diced pumpkin into a bowl. Add remaining filling ingredients, stir to combine, cover, and leave for 12 hours or overnight.
3. Sift flour and salt into a mixing bowl. Add oil and rub in with fingertips until distributed evenly. Add water and lemon juice; mix to form a firm dough.
4. Knead lightly, cover, and let rest for 30 minutes.
5. Roll out the dough thinly (about the thickness of a normal pie crust) and cut into 15 cm (6 inch) rounds.
6. Place a round of pastry on a lightly greased baking tray. Moisten the edges with water.
7. Add a tablespoonful of filling in the center, fold over, and press edges to seal well. Flute the edge with fingers or press with fork tines.
8. Brush tops of pies with egg beaten with a little milk.
9. Bake in a hot oven for 10 minutes, then reduce temperature to moderate and bake for an additional 20 minutes.
10. Serve hot or cold.
